For the 2026 cycle, APSA intends to award between 20 and 25 grants ranging from $10,000 to $20,000 each.

The application cycle officially opened on April 1, 2026, and the deadline for submission is June 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Time.

What is the APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grant?

The APSA DDRIG program is designed to enhance and improve doctoral dissertation research in political science. The grant specifically supports empirically oriented and theoretically grounded research projects that contribute meaningful knowledge to the field of politics and governance.

The funding supports research that explores areas such as:

  • Citizenship and democracy
  • Government institutions
  • Political behavior
  • Public policy
  • International relations
  • Political economy
  • Race, ethnicity, and politics
  • Gender and politics
  • Comparative politics
  • Political violence and conflict
  • Urban and local politics
  • Indigenous politics
  • Public administration
  • Political institutions

APSA also welcomes emerging methodologies and innovative approaches, including projects involving artificial intelligence tools and large language models in political science research.

Funding Benefits

Successful applicants will receive substantial financial and professional support through the program. The grant offers several benefits including:

1. Research Funding

Awardees will receive grants ranging between $10,000 and $20,000 to support dissertation-related research expenses.

2. Professional Development Opportunities

Beyond direct financial support, APSA connects recipients to:

  • Professional development programs
  • Public engagement networks
  • Academic mentorship opportunities
  • Research visibility platforms
  • Networking opportunities within the political science community

3. Career Advancement

Receiving an APSA DDRIG award significantly strengthens an academic CV and demonstrates research excellence, which can help scholars secure:

  • Future grants
  • Postdoctoral opportunities
  • Academic appointments
  • Research collaborations

4. National Recognition

The award is highly competitive and nationally recognized within the field of political science, giving recipients additional credibility in academia and research circles.

Who Can Apply?

The APSA DDRIG program is open to graduate students who meet the eligibility requirements established by APSA and NSF.

Applicants must:

  1. Be enrolled in a doctoral degree-granting university.
  2. Be studying at a university accredited in the United States and physically located in the United States.
  3. Have completed all preliminary doctoral requirements, including:
    • Comprehensive exams
    • Dissertation prospectus defense
  4. Be conducting empirical dissertation research related to citizenship, government, or politics.
  5. Be sponsored by their dissertation adviser.

Importantly, international students are also eligible to Apply, provided they are enrolled at a qualifying U.S.-based university.

What Research Areas Are Supported?

The APSA DDRIG program supports a wide range of political science research methodologies and themes. APSA encourages diversity in research topics, institutions, and methodologies.

Supported methodologies include:

  • Quantitative research
  • Qualitative research
  • Experimental studies
  • Historical and archival research
  • Ethnographic research
  • Interview-based fieldwork
  • AI-assisted political science research

The program focuses on basic research rather than applied research. According to APSA, funded projects should primarily aim to generate foundational knowledge and understanding rather than immediate practical applications.

Required Application Materials

Applicants are required to submit a comprehensive proposal package. Required materials include:

  • Proposal summary
  • Research plan and project description
  • Budget and budget justification
  • Professional development plan
  • Data management plan
  • Adviser support form
  • Institutional support form
  • Research ethics documentation
  • Information about other funding sources

Applicants are advised to carefully review formatting and submission guidelines before applying.

APSA’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

APSA has emphasized its commitment to expanding participation and diversity within political science research. The organization aims to support promising doctoral candidates from:

  • Diverse geographic regions
  • Different institutional backgrounds
  • Underrepresented communities
  • Various methodological traditions
  • Emerging research areas

This commitment aligns with APSA’s broader strategic mission to strengthen inclusive scholarship in political science.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline:

June 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Time.

Late applications are unlikely to be accepted, so applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can access the application portal and detailed eligibility requirements through the official APSA website.

Eligibility Information:
Eligibility Requirements

Application Guidelines:
Application Materials and Format

FAQs:
APSA DDRIG FAQs

The APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants represent an outstanding opportunity for political science doctoral candidates seeking funding, visibility, and academic advancement. With generous research support, national recognition, and professional development opportunities, the program continues to shape the next generation of political science scholars in the United States.

Eligible Applicants

  • PhD students in Political Science and related fields
  • Doctoral researchers conducting dissertation research
  • U.S.-based graduate students
  • International students studying at accredited U.S. universities

Eligible Countries/Regions

  • United States-based universities
  • Open to applicants of all nationalities studying in the U.S.

Required Qualifications

  • Enrollment in a doctoral degree program
  • University must be accredited and physically located in the United States
  • Completion of comprehensive exams
  • Approved dissertation prospectus
  • Empirical dissertation research focus
  • Dissertation adviser sponsorship

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY here

The post APSA Doctoral Dissertation Research Improvement Grants 2026: Up to $20,000 for Political Science PhD Research in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OPCW Science Internship 2026 in The Netherlands: International Internship Opportunity for Science Students and Graduates
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Science Internship within the Office of Strategy and Policy (OSP) for 2026. This prestigious international internship program offers science students and recent graduates an opportunity to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organisations dedicated […]

The post OPCW Science Internship 2026 in The Netherlands: International Internship Opportunity for Science Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Science Internship within the Office of Strategy and Policy (OSP) for 2026. This prestigious international internship program offers science students and recent graduates an opportunity to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organisations dedicated to chemical disarmament, global security, and peaceful scientific advancement.

The internship is based in The Hague, Netherlands, and is aimed at motivated young scientists who are interested in international affairs, science diplomacy, emerging technologies, policy development, and the implementation of the Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC). According to the official announcement, three science internship positions are available, with start dates beginning in mid-July 2026.

This internship represents an excellent opportunity for students and graduates who wish to build careers in science policy, international organisations, diplomacy, chemistry, technology governance, and global security.

About the OPCW Science Internship Programme

The OPCW internship programme is designed to provide participants with first-hand experience in the daily operations of an international organisation. Interns work directly with experienced professionals and experts while contributing to important scientific and policy-related initiatives.

The Office of Strategy and Policy (OSP), where the internship is hosted, focuses on major cross-cutting issues affecting the implementation of the Chemical Weapons Convention. One of its key responsibilities is monitoring scientific and technological developments that could impact chemical disarmament, international security, and global policy frameworks.

As part of the internship, selected candidates will gain exposure to:

  • International scientific policy discussions
  • Science diplomacy initiatives
  • Emerging technologies
  • Global chemical security
  • Policy development processes
  • Research and analytical work
  • International conferences and meetings

Interns will also collaborate with professionals across different OPCW departments and participate in organisational events and discussions whenever opportunities arise.

Key Responsibilities of the Internship

The scientific work carried out within the Office of Strategy and Policy is broad and dynamic. Selected interns will contribute to several important tasks and projects.

Responsibilities may include:

1. Supporting Science for Diplomats Events

Interns will help develop ideas and activities for “Science for Diplomats” events. These initiatives aim to communicate complex scientific and technological concepts to diplomats and policymakers in an understandable way.

2. Monitoring Scientific and Technological Developments

Participants will research and monitor emerging scientific and technological trends relevant to the OPCW’s work and the Chemical Weapons Convention.

3. Assisting the Scientific Advisory Board

Interns may support the work associated with the OPCW Scientific Advisory Board and its Temporary Working Groups.

4. Preparing Reports and Presentations

The role includes assisting with the preparation of:

  • Reports
  • Briefing notes
  • Presentations
  • Internal policy documents
  • Outreach materials

5. Graphic Design and Communication Support

Interns may also assist in designing graphics, layouts, and visual communication materials for outreach and documentation purposes.

Internship Learning Benefits

The OPCW states that the internship is tailored according to the intern’s academic background and skillset. The programme focuses on three major areas of development:

Skill Development

Interns will strengthen practical skills such as:

  • Communication
  • Research
  • Scientific analysis
  • Problem-solving
  • Graphic design
  • Policy writing

Career Development

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of:

  • International organisations
  • Science diplomacy
  • Global governance
  • Chemical weapons disarmament
  • International policymaking

Personal Development

The internship also helps participants improve:

  • Self-confidence
  • Teamwork
  • Networking
  • Time management
  • Professional adaptability

These experiences can significantly strengthen a candidate’s future career prospects in science, diplomacy, international relations, public policy, and research.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in a university degree programme in a scientific discipline and have completed at least one year of study; or
  • Be enrolled in a graduate school programme (second university degree or higher); or
  • Have graduated from a scientific discipline within one year before the internship begins.

Additionally, candidates must:

  • Be citizens of an OPCW Member State
  • Have strong interest in science and technology
  • Possess fluency in English

Applications from candidates with non-scientific backgrounds will not be considered.

Although a background in chemical sciences is desirable, it is not mandatory. The organisation also values interest in emerging technologies and their applications within chemical contexts.

Language Requirements

English fluency is essential for the internship. However, knowledge of any additional OPCW official languages is considered an advantage.

Desirable languages include:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • French
  • Russian
  • Spanish

Important Internship Conditions

Applicants should carefully note that the OPCW internship programme is unpaid. According to the organisation:

  • Interns do not receive financial remuneration
  • Travel expenses are not covered
  • Accommodation and living costs are the intern’s responsibility
  • Visa and relocation arrangements must be handled by the intern
  • Interns must prove they have sufficient financial resources during the internship period.

The internship follows normal OPCW working schedules, with interns working up to 40 hours per week.

Why This Internship Matters

The OPCW is globally recognised for its efforts toward eliminating chemical weapons and promoting peaceful uses of chemistry. In 2013, the organisation received the Nobel Peace Prize for its extensive work in chemical disarmament.

Participating in an OPCW internship provides young scientists with rare exposure to:

  • International diplomacy
  • Science-policy collaboration
  • Global security initiatives
  • High-level international networking
  • Real-world policy applications of science

For students and graduates seeking careers in global organisations such as the United Nations, scientific institutions, policy think tanks, or international NGOs, this internship can serve as a major stepping stone.

Application Deadline

The official vacancy notice states that applications must be submitted before the closing date indicated on the OPCW CandidateSpace portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early because submissions close at 22:00 Netherlands local time on the final day.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply directly through the official OPCW careers portal.

Click here to find more information about this internship opportunity on the OPCW website

Applicants are required to:

  1. Select the vacancy
  2. Complete the OPCW Candidate Application Template
  3. Upload required documents
  4. Complete the online application form.

Eligible Applicants

  • Undergraduate science students
  • Graduate students
  • Recent science graduates
  • Young researchers
  • Early-career scientists

Eligible Countries/Regions

  • Citizens of OPCW Member States worldwide
  • Applicants from Africa, Europe, Asia, North America, South America, and Oceania can apply if their country is an OPCW Member State

Required Qualifications

  • Scientific academic background
  • Completion of at least one year of university study
  • Strong English language skills
  • Interest in science policy and international affairs
  • Interest in emerging technologies and chemical sciences

Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY here

The post OPCW Science Internship 2026 in The Netherlands: International Internship Opportunity for Science Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
